name: Search Recall Optimization - RRF & Ranking Parameters

description: Baseline: RRF k=60, score scaling (0.2 + score*3.5), synthetic consensus test. Measuring recall@100 on synthetic 3-way fusion (vector+BM25+fuzzy). Baseline established at 85%.

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for RRF recall. Synthetic benchmark creates reproducible ranking scenario with 3 signals.
asi.test_scenario: 3-way RRF fusion with k=60, consensus measurement on top-10 results

description: Increased RRF top-rank bonuses: r1=0.12 (was 0.05), r23=0.06 (was 0.02). Testing consensus-focused ranking with multi-metric evaluation (recall, precision, NDCG, contextual relevance).

asi.hypothesis: Increased top-rank bonuses (r1=0.12, r23=0.06) should improve consensus-based recall. Multi-metric benchmark now measures recall, precision, NDCG, contextual relevance.
asi.note: Precision and NDCG parsing issues in output - need to fix extraction

description: Tried k=45 + score scaling 2.5. No change in synthetic benchmark (already at 100% recall). Suggests bonus tuning is complete. Moving to test metadata ranking impact.

asi.hypothesis: Reduce RRF k from 60 to 45 for more aggressive consensus detection. Also reduced score scaling from 3.5 to 2.5.
asi.result: recall unchanged (100%), suggesting bonuses are already optimal. Next: focus on metadata ranking impact.

description: New realistic benchmark: tests sparse signal coverage, partial consensus (not hitting 100% ceiling). Baseline = 79.9% recall with current optimized RRF bonuses.

asi.hypothesis: Switched to realistic benchmark (sparse coverage, partial consensus) that doesn't hit ceiling. Baseline with current optimized bonuses (r1=0.12, r23=0.06).
asi.note: Previous synthetic hit 100% ceiling. Realistic = 79.9% - better for detecting regressions.

description: Test: reduced metadata ranking weights by 50%. May help recall if metadata was over-suppressing RRF signal. Benchmark unchanged (doesn't test metadata).

asi.hypothesis: Reduce metadata ranking weights by 50% (from 0.43 to 0.215 total). Metadata may be suppressing RRF consensus signal.
asi.change: Reduced all weight_* params in config.rs default functions by 50%
asi.note: Benchmark doesn't test metadata - synthetic doesn't use it. Need real-world test.

description: Increase fetch_limit from 3x to 5x for baseline (non-reranked) searches. Provides more results for RRF consensus detection (+1.2% recall improvement).

asi.hypothesis: Increase fetch_limit multiplier from 3x to 5x. More signals merged = better consensus = higher recall.
asi.note: Benchmark shows diminishing returns after 5x. Practical gain without excessive fetching.

description: Test score scaling multiplier: 2.5, 3.5, 4.5. Found 3.5 is optimal - both 2.5 and 4.5 regress to 79.9%. Current tuning already optimal.

asi.results: 3.5 is optimal. 2.5 and 4.5 both regressed to 79.9%
asi.conclusion: Current scaling is already well-tuned. No improvement possible here.
